acceptance.reason: total_train_sec ratio is 38x (dominated by first-epoch warmup); steady-state ratio is ~7x

task: MNIST addition (5 epochs, 512 train images, batch_size=64)

xlog.source: re-run with real epoch_times from TrainingHistory

notes: Previous 44x ratio was an artifact of approximating all epoch times as equal.
Real breakdown: first epoch is 67-78s (CUDA JIT + circuit cache warmup), epochs 2-5 are ~2.6s each.
Steady-state per-query cost is ~10.3ms (XLOG) vs ~1.5ms (Scallop) = 7x ratio.
First-epoch warmup is a one-time cost that amortizes over more epochs.
At 50 epochs: projected XLOG ~202s vs Scallop ~19s = ~10.6x ratio.
Accuracy difference is within noise at this 5-epoch training budget.
Remaining steady-state bottleneck is sequential per-query circuit evaluation (10ms/query).

hardware: NVIDIA RTX PRO 3000 Blackwell Generation Laptop GPU (12 GB, SM120)
